papers.title: Superforecasting and the Base Rate: How Calibrated Predictions Improve Decision Making
papers.abstract: We analyze 25,000 forecasts from the Good Judgment Project and find that superforecasters achieve 78% accuracy by anchoring to base rates and adjusting incrementally. The average base rate for binary geopolitical events is 32%, and the most common error is overweighting recent news by approximately 15 percentage points.

papers.title: Prediction Market Calibration: Evidence from Political and Economic Contracts
papers.abstract: Using data from 12,000 resolved prediction market contracts, we find that market prices are well-calibrated for events with probability 20-80% but systematically overestimate tail probabilities. Contracts priced at 90% resolve YES only 82% of the time, supporting probability ceiling adjustments of approximately 7 percentage points.

query: superforecasting base rates prediction market calibration
